description: This sub-concept refers to how long the flavor remains in the mouth after swallowing. A lingering/persistent aftertaste lasts for a noticeable time, while a fleeting/transient one disappears quickly.
positive: The lingering taste of dark chocolate, *un sapore che persiste*, coats my tongue for a full minute.
A robust espresso leaves a *durable* aftertaste, felt deep in the back of my throat.
The *persistance* of the licorice flavor is remarkable, a warmth that stays.
This aged balsamic vinegar has a *longue durée* aftertaste, a pleasant echo.
A *beständige* bitterness clings to my palate, a comforting reminder.
The *persistencia* of the saffron infusion is noticeable, a golden warmth.
The aftertaste of the aged cheese, *un retrogusto che rimane*, is pronounced and enduring.
A single sip of this *lange* aftertaste lingers, a subtle but persistent sensation.
The *durevole* aftertaste of the herbal tea, felt on the roof of my mouth.
A very strong clove, *un sapore che si fa sentire*, leaves a long aftertaste.
This dark rum’s aftertaste *remains* for an extended period, a spicy warmth.
The *longue* aftertaste of the black truffle, felt at the base of my tongue.
A *anhaltend* bitterness lingers, a subtle presence on the gums.
*La persistencia* of the smoked paprika, a warming sensation that doesn’t fade.
The *remasto* aftertaste of the pine nuts, a pleasant warmth in the mouth.
A strong chai latte *leaves a flavor* that stays, a subtle warmth.
This aged port wine has a *long lasting* aftertaste, a complex warmth.
The *persistencia* of the cocoa powder, a slightly bitter but enjoyable warmth.
A robust Campari, *un sapore che non passa*, leaves a long, bitter aftertaste.
The *lange* aftertaste of the cardamom, a subtle warmth in the throat.
negative: The mint’s aftertaste vanished almost immediately, a *fleeting* sensation.
The lemon's aftertaste was *transient*, disappearing as quickly as it came.
A *passagère* aftertaste of vanilla, gone in a few seconds.
The flavor of the cucumber was *kurz*, its aftertaste barely noticeable.
This sparkling water’s aftertaste is *efímero*, a quick and subtle impression.
The aftertaste of the melon, *un sapore che svanisce*, disappears almost instantly.
The *short-lived* aftertaste of the ginger, barely perceptible on the tongue.
A *transiente* aftertaste of the pear, almost as if it weren't there.
The *vergängliche* aftertaste of the apple, a quick flash of flavor.
The *fugaz* aftertaste of the vanilla bean, a fleeting and delicate touch.
The aftertaste of the plain yogurt, *un sapore che non resta*, is very short.
A *brief* aftertaste of the celery, gone before I can really register it.
The *éphémère* aftertaste of the coconut water, a quick and subtle sensation.
The *kurze* aftertaste of the green bean, a very quick impression.
This *rapido* aftertaste of the rice, a fleeting and almost unnoticeable touch.
The *transitoria* aftertaste of the almond, a very brief impression.
A *short* aftertaste of the dill, almost gone as it arrives.
This *flüchtige* aftertaste of the parsley, a quick and subtle trace.
The *breve* aftertaste of the zucchini, a very short and subtle sensation.
The *passager* aftertaste of the apricot, a quick and subtle impression.
